title: Number Theory v3: Langlands Program, L-Functions & Motives

zeta.note: ζ(s) for s>1: EML-2 (power law sum); critical line: EML-3; RH: EML-∞

modularity.note: Local Langlands (GL_n proved)=EML-2; global=EML-∞; FLT proof=EML-3

l_functions.key_insight: L-functions=EML-3; local Langlands=EML-2; global=EML-∞; critical line=EML-3

pure_motives.note: Motives: Betti=EML-0; de Rham=EML-2; ℓ-adic=EML-2; motivic category=EML-∞

p_adic.note: p-adic valuation=EML-2; p-adic L-function=EML-3; Iwasawa main conj=EML-∞

motives.key_insight: Motives: realizations=EML-2; category=EML-∞; Langlands ladder GL(1)→GL(2)→GL(n)

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: Betti numbers (cohomology), topological invariants of varieties
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Local Langlands (proved), ζ(s) convergent, de Rham/ℓ-adic realizations
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Global Langlands (GL(n≥3)), geometric Langlands, motivic category, RH, GRH

key_theorem: The EML Number Theory v3 Theorem (S208): The Langlands program spans the EML ladder: Local Langlands (GL(n) proved) = EML-2 (proved = structural EML-2). Global GL(1) (class field theory) = EML-2. Global GL(2) / FLT (Wiles) = EML-3 (elliptic curve L-function oscillation). Global GL(n≥3) / Geometric Langlands = EML-∞ (open Millennium-level problems). Motives: all geometric realizations = EML-2; motivic category conjectures = EML-∞. Depth ladder within Langlands: EML-2 → EML-3 → EML-∞ as n increases.
rabbit_hole_log: Langlands EML ladder: local(2) → GL(2)(3) → GL(n)(∞): depth increases with n
All motivic realizations = EML-2: de Rham, ℓ-adic, crystalline — universal EML-2
GRH = EML-∞: same depth as BSD, NS, confinement — Horizon theorem confirmed
